message:
fix(interactive): complete try_send → enqueue_pi_event migration across session, auth, and bash command flows

The previous commits (cc97fa51, ab3ac638) migrated extension host action
events to the backpressure-aware enqueue_pi_event helper, but several
call sites in the session-switching, credential-login, and bash-command
paths still used the raw try_send API. Under channel backpressure (e.g.
many concurrent extensions firing), try_send silently drops events,
causing the UI to lose error messages, credential-update confirmations,
and bash output.

This commit converts every remaining try_send(PiMsg::...) in the
interactive layer to enqueue_pi_event(&event_tx, &Cx::for_request(), ...),
ensuring consistent backpressure handling:

- src/interactive.rs: session lock failures, session-switch cancellation,
  ConversationReset after session resume
- src/interactive/agent.rs: extension command/shortcut done events, agent
  and session lock failures, input-blocked and error events during message
  submission, save-error and agent-error propagation, UpdateLastUserMessage
- src/interactive/commands.rs: auth storage load errors, credential save
  errors, CredentialUpdated events, login status system messages, bash
  result delivery (both success and failure), session-switch cancellation,
  session lock failures during /session command

Also updates Cx construction from Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(Cx::for_request)
to the simpler Cx::for_request() in agent.rs, matching the pattern already
established in the prior fixes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
